facade_colour: dark charcoal grey painted stucco with black trim; large blue spruce tree at front

hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain many of their original building features including such as their mirrored façades, shared roofline with central gable, brick cladding, first storey bay windows, and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains supporting the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using fixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, bay window, cornice.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain many of their original building features including such as their mirrored façades, shared roofline with central gable, brick cladding, first storey bay windows, and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.

facade_description: Semi-detached two-storey house-form in dark charcoal grey painted stucco with black trim; large blue spruce tree at front with a gable roof. Features include a double-height projecting bay, decorative bargeboards and a simple cornice. Complete exterior renovation; dark grey stucco; modern windows; original details hidden. Constructed 1890-1903.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain many of their original building features including such as their mirrored façades, shared roofline with central gable, brick cladding, first storey bay windows, and fenestration pattern with segmentally arched openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.
